Composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the backend code in a single Python file (e.g., app.py) that 
- Runs the website on local port 5000 with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard page).
- Implements routing for all 10 frontend pages as per the requirements document.
- Handles file read/write operations on all data files in the 'data' directory using plain text starting from the first line.
- Supports all functionalities including browsing destinations, planning itineraries, searching accommodations, booking flights, viewing travel packages, managing trips, booking confirmations, and travel recommendations.
- Ensures no authentication is required; all features are directly accessible.
- Lists all frontend code files (HTML templates) corresponding to the 10 pages, which will be developed based on this backend.
Composition.Programmer 2:
Develop the frontend code (HTML templates) for all 10 pages 
- Dashboard Page (dashboard.html)
- Destinations Page (destinations.html)
- Destination Details Page (destination_details.html)
- Itinerary Planning Page (itinerary.html)
- Accommodations Page (accommodations.html)
- Transportation Page (transportation.html)
- Travel Packages Page (packages.html)
- Trip Management Page (trips.html)
- Booking Confirmation Page (confirmation.html)
- Travel Recommendations Page (recommendations.html)
- All URLs in the frontend code should be implemented as routes, not static HTML file names.
- Each page must include the specified elements with their IDs and types as per the requirements document.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of ALL pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of all specified elements on each page as per the requirements

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
